mentions.note: Azure guidance came in light but capex reversal and OpenAI's $250B Azure commitment keep Cramer a believer.
Stock punished on heavy AI capex spending, now down from $555 to $485 — an uncommon chance to buy a quality AI compounder.
Down 6% YTD; high-quality tech being sold; consider trimming if overweighted.
Down almost 6% YTD as quality tech underperforms speculative names; fundamentals solid but rotation pressuring stock.
Magnificent 7 member; money temporarily flowing to storage plays but company has too much cash and smart leadership; patience needed.
Weak partly because all software under pressure from generative AI concern (false worry in MSFT case); shaved P/E multiple. Very difficult to own except select days.

Down 13% since October report after CapEx surprise reversal (now expects growth higher FY2026 vs FY2025). Need no new CapEx increases and Azure upside (39% vs 36% expected) plus Copilot/Windows enterprise clarity Wednesday.

Reports Wednesday. Watch CapEx guidance (no incremental increases desired) and Azure growth upside (39% vs. expected 36%). Copilot and Windows moat questions linger. Down 13% since last report; need reassurance on spending trajectory.
Beat Q4 on top/bottom line but capex +66% YoY on Azure AI investment worried Wall Street; stock sold off hard after-hours.
Scissors this quarter on weak Copilot adoption (1% of users) and insufficient AI capex, but smart leadership will fix it next quarter; waiting for velocity decline to dry up before re-buying.
Least magnificent of Mag 7; convoluted conference call; lost AI race to GOOGL; OpenAI relationship undefined/unclear; weak Copilot uptake at 15M users; felt sorry for MSFT.
Down 3% on AI disruption fears; already been hammered. Unclear if selloff has legs or is pure panic.
Azure decelerated shipping chips elsewhere; Copilot demand weak (15M paid users pitiful); now 24x earnings—more enticing.
Cramer 'very worried' about CAPEX spending on AI infrastructure; considering selling some or all despite trust owning it—huge change for him.
Enterprise software incumbent under siege from AI upstarts like OpenAI, Anthropic, Databricks; despite profitability, facing existential competitive threat.
Once-loved now disliked; great performer historically; no catch bid until rates stop rising; hold for recovery.
Dominates Windows with captive office audience and mandatory Copilot pushes yet stock can't move higher—palpable sense something very wrong; on borrowed time in charitable trust.
Largest software component of IGV down; empire being toppled by hardware and AI; ironically Microsoft now vulnerable despite AI investments.

Jennifer's portfolio: too close to SAP; both struggling; doing terribly trading-wise (companies okay fundamentally but stock action poor); Cramer recommends swap out for J&J and RTX to preserve capital.
Down 35% from July peak on AI displacement; OpenAI pushing customers to Amazon; not looking like AI net winner despite today's 3.6% bounce.
Behind on AI; could be hurt by Anthropic and OpenAI competition. Business growth not as strong as hoped, but balance sheet strength allows for strategic pivots if management acts.
Rotation play; soared 4.6% with no news as leaders cool; new management engagement showing urgency reassures shareholders about AI evisceration fears being overblown.
Azure cloud infrastructure layer for AI; third-layer provider in Jensen Huang's ecosystem model.

Copilot concerns overdone; Azure cloud infrastructure robust, Windows franchise second to none. Citi confirms strong reseller demand and terrific Azure results. Stock up $6 to 424 after recovery bounce started late March.
Can surprise with spectacular data center numbers; Microsoft doing buyouts; co-pilot demand remains not so hot but data center upside significant.
Mega-cap tech earnings tomorrow; serial underperformer (fell 10% on earnings day recently); capex guidance critical. Held in trust.
Disappointing quarter relative to expectations; software play lacks excitement vs. shortage plays; could consider on pullback if dividend-laden, but Cramer prefers Ford.

18% revenue growth, 23% EPS growth, Azure +40% strong, but overall revenue guidance light, Copilot users 20M underwhelming, and $40B+ quarterly CapEx with 190B full-year forecast spooked market; weak narrative on how spending converts to returns; stock down 4%.
Stuck with old software fallen out of favor; lagging despite Azure scale; structural disadvantage vs. Amazon Web Services and Google Cloud.
Even MSFT pressured due to software sector bias; cloud/infrastructure not enough to offset software stigma.
Software/applications beneficiary; up today on Data Dog rotation; huge presence in IGV ETF.
Led software rotation up 3% but move feels like giant short squeeze; all prior enterprise software rallies reversed after strong initial days.
Traditional software losing pricing power and primacy; AI portion potentially strong but Windows/software model threatened by AI alternatives; dominance era ending.
Terrific cloud business offset by weak enterprise software and disappointing Copilot AI product; must prove execution capability or stock will have to go.

Stagnant price while AI-enabled peers explode creates frustrating opportunity cost; thought to be cheap but proving value trap; pending sell decision if Q2 disappoints; better AI beneficiaries elsewhere.
Microsoft software rebound suggests market returning to quality names; fundamental strength reasserting after punishment
Collaborating with Nvidia on ARM-based superchips for AI agents; stock gained 2%; key beneficiary of data center capex cycle.
Down 17% in June; software business losing favor on CapEx spending; needs bold action: acquire AI company, break up divisions, or replace Copilot with ChatGPT.

Hallowed stock usually trades with Mag 7 but lately trading with defensive enterprise software. Cramer bullish on low prices as gift; trying to hold for trust despite prior self-flagellation. Stock benefiting from hardware-to-software rotation.
Magnificent 7 member; secular growth in cloud and AI with strong margins and capital efficiency; observable leader in enterprise.
